items.title: Notre Dame university halts new nursing student enrolments for another year

children.content: The university says it will not take new students for its nursing program for another year in a bid to "rebalance enrolments" after becoming oversubscribed, leaving many students in limbo.

gallery.altText: A sign for the University of Notre Dame at its Fremantle campus
Generic shot of a nurse testing unidentified patient's blood sugar.

thumbnail.altText: A sign for the University of Notre Dame at its Fremantle campus
Generic shot of a nurse testing unidentified patient's blood sugar.

items.title: Damning report finds WA prisoners subjected to 'cruel, degrading treatment'

image.altText: Casuarina Prison barbed wire sunset

children.content: WA prisoners are being subjected to "cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment" because the state government has allowed prisons to become "almost fundamentally broken", according to the Inspector of Custodial Services.

items.title: John Worsfold, Gary Ablett Jr added to Australian Football Hall of Fame

children.content: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including West Coast premiership captain and coach John Worsfold and Geelong and Gold Coast great Gary Ablett Jr.

gallery.altText: John Worsfold poses with his Australian Football Hall of Fame inductee certificate.
Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

thumbnail.altText: John Worsfold poses with his Australian Football Hall of Fame inductee certificate.
Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

items.title: Former WA Police officer 'gutted' after compensation for horrific injury denied

children.content: Former WA Police officer and WA Australian of the Year Paul Litherland says he is "heartbroken" after his claim for post-service medical compensation over injuries he received in the force was denied.

image.altText: Profile shot of Paul Litherland

items.title: Incorrect Medicare billing allegations spark concerns of broader issue

children.content: One of Australia's largest medical imaging providers, Integral Diagnostics, says it will look into allegations of improper Medicare billing at a regional WA public hospital where the company provides radiology services.

image.altText: A hand holds a medicare card

items.title: New leader, old problem for WA Liberals as Zempilas flirts with One Nation

children.content: As Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation, will the right-wing populist party drag the WA Liberals down again, or will it become a life raft on the way to a political resurrection?

articles.title: Ignoring One Nation like 'ignoring will of people', Basil Zempilas warns

children.content: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas says One Nation deserves "respect" and he is open to finding a way of working with the right-wing populist party as support for it grows.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas stands at a podium and addresses the crowd

gallery.altText: A close up of the torso of a man in a suit surrounded by people
A woman speaks to the camera
A man in a navy cap wears a bright orange shirt with the words  "We've got the guts to say what you're thinking" on the back.

A woman speaks to the camera
A man in a navy cap wears a bright orange shirt with the words  "We've got the guts to say what you're thinking" on the back.

items.title: Why was Rolf Harris never tried for his crimes in Australia?

children.content: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

items.title: Thirteen government agencies embroiled in potential data breach

children.content: Thirteen government agencies are now embroiled in an expanding scandal involving potential data breaches prompting calls for an urgent audit amid concerns over national security.

image.altText: A stack of documents sitting in an office, generic.

items.title: How police may have inadvertently saved this man from Ivan Milat

children.content: Etched in Steven Clark's memory are the protruding teeth and moustache of the driver he says picked him up as a teenager — a man he now believes was serial killer Ivan Milat.

image.altText: Man wearing a hat stands on the side of a road.

items.title: Is Australia sucking the joy out of childhood?

children.content: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: play time.

image.altText: Two young white kids holding hands, running through long grass

items.title: Quiz: Stow your luggage in the locker overhead, we're off to see the world

children.content: The lek is the currency of which country? And what's the Japanese word for pufferfish, the sashimi delicacy that's toxic if prepared incorrectly?

image.altText: Chop sticks hover over a plat of thinly sliced raw white fish with a green garnish.

items.title: Residents shocked by severe storm as South West WA homes left flooded

children.content: An unexpected deluge floods homes and public facilities in the South West WA town of Harvey, with the community pelted with 40 millimetres of rain in one hour.

items.title: Calling all budding storytellers living with disability

children.content: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

image.altText: two women stand alongside one another smiling. One of the women has her right arm amputated. They stand in an ABC studio.

items.title: Victim-survivors tear up agreement with Catholic church

children.content: Victim-survivors of clergy abuse say negotiations and agreements reached with the Catholic Diocese of Ballarat are now null and void.

image.altText: Colourful ribbons on a church ground fence

items.title: Serena Williams makes winning return to court at Queen's

children.content: Serena Williams steals the show at Queen's Club, as the American icon marks her eagerly awaited return to competitive action with a doubles victory nearly four years after her last match.

image.altText: Serena Williams celebrates a point with Victoria Mboko at Queen's.

items.title: Kyrgios wins Stuttgart opener after five-month tour absence

children.content: Nick Kyrgios makes a successful return to the court, claiming only his second singles victory since 2022.

image.altText: Nick Kyrgios prepares to play a forehand on the run at Stuttgart ATP Tour event.

items.title: When does the FIFA World Cup start and who are the favourites?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

articles.title: Somalian referee returns home after being denied entry to US for World Cup

children.content: Omar Artan thanks FIFA for its support after he is barred from entering the United States, ending his dream of refereeing at his first World Cup.

image.altText: Referee Omar Artan gestures during the FIFA U-20 World Cup in 2025.

articles.title: Socceroos are a long way from home but have a special Oakland connection

children.content: Socceroos captain Mat Ryan is a long way from home, but he has found an unexpected connection in Oakland.

image.altText: Jesus De Vincente of the Oakland Roots Soccer Club and Mat Ryan of the Socceroos pose for a photo

articles.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you

children.content: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.

items.title: These cranes are battery-powered as firms increasingly ditch diesel

children.content: The Iran war and the oil supply squeeze are supercharging business investment in renewables.

image.altText: Crane in the centre of a large construction site. City buildings in the background.

items.title: Steaks could soon cost more, but restaurants have other ideas

children.content: Red meat is expected to become more expensive starting this month as higher diesel prices have filtered through the supply chain.

image.altText: A juicy rib steak on a bone served on a restaurant plate with a sophisticated salad and glaze.

items.title: From the Kimberley to Sweden, how heartbreak helped me take a travel leap

children.content: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

gallery.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

thumbnail.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

items.title: 'Hidden cameras' captured Perth paediatrician's alleged sexual offending, court hears

children.content: A paediatrician charged with child sex offences may have up to nine victims, with alleged offending captured on "hidden cameras" in consulting rooms, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A close up of a man wearing glasses and a suit

items.title: Aussies record first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 years

children.content: Bangladesh posts just its second victory over Australia in 23 ODIs, recording an 86-run win in the opening match of the series in Dhaka.

image.altText: Liam Scott reacts while batting for Australia against Bangladesh in a 2026 ODI.

items.title: Calls for kindness as community mourns death of shark attack victim

children.content: The Albany community is still coming to terms with the loss of resident Daniel Turpin after he did not survive a shark attack at the weekend.

articles.title: Daniel Turpin named as diver killed in WA shark attack

children.content: The family of a 35-year-old diver who died after being bitten by a shark has described him as "an adored husband, son, brother and uncle who had a lifelong love and deep respect for the ocean".
